## From: 24-sprint-plan-20260510-20260517.md

- `[ ]` Observability standards Claude hook — write a `.claude/hooks/observability-check.py` post-edit hook (or extend `post-edit.py`) that warns when backend edits add a new LLM call without an OTel span, a new FastAPI endpoint without `require_api_key` / `get_current_user`, or a new structlog call using `logging.getLogger` instead of `structlog.get_logger`. Advisory output, non-blocking. (from "## Backlog")
- `[ ]` LinkedIn URL missing protocol — `authorLinkedin` values stored as `linkedin.com/in/username` render as relative paths in `CandidateFooter`. Fix: (1) normalize at render with `startsWith('http') ? url : 'https://${url}'` in `CandidateFooter`; (2) strip protocol/www at extraction in `profile_extractor.py` and always prepend `https://` at render. (from "## Backlog")
- `[~]` Reorder chunks within a group — `position` field is in the PATCH contract and save loop, but no drag/arrow UI built; deferred to next session (from "### Analysis View — Editor Mode")
- `[~]` Merge chunks — `POST /tailorings/{id}/chunks/merge` endpoint + route done; no selection UI built in `ChunkItem`; deferred (from "### Analysis View — Editor Mode")
- `[~]` Design edit surface for the generated letter — deferred out of scope for Day 21 (from "### Letter View Controls")

## From: 28-conversational-experience-agent.md (entire feature unbuilt)

- `[ ]` Phase 1 — Quick Log UI on `/dashboard/experience`: textarea + confirmation flow, extend `ParsedClaims` with `suggested_group_key: str | None` and `needs_attribution: bool`, attribution inferred from text signals ("at Acme Corp" → `group_key = "Acme Corp"`); Review state shows editable claim list + group_key field (from "## Phase 1: Text-to-Experience" + "## Sequencing")
- `[ ]` Phase 2a — `ConversationThread` + `ConversationMessage` SQLAlchemy models + Alembic migration; one thread per user for now; last 15 messages as LLM context (from "## Phase 2 — 2b. DB Schema")
- `[ ]` Phase 2b — `POST /chat` endpoint: `IntentClassification` LLM call (add_experience / create_tailoring / query / confirmation / rejection / unclear), `add_experience` handler wired to existing parse + chunk endpoints (from "## Sequencing")
- `[ ]` Phase 2c — Frontend chat panel: floating button → slide-out overlay on all dashboard pages; optimistic message display; thread ID in localStorage (from "## Frontend Architecture" + "## Sequencing")
- `[ ]` Phase 2d — `create_tailoring` intent handler: extract URL → kick off tailoring pipeline → return immediately → write follow-up `ConversationMessage` when generation_status = "ready"; frontend polls for new messages (from "## Sequencing")
- `[ ]` Phase 2e — `query` intent handler: profile Q&A using formatted profile as LLM context; no tool calls (from "## Sequencing")
- `[ ]` Attribution clarification loop (Phase 3): two-turn max; agent asks for employer/project when no attribution can be inferred; saves with `group_key = null` on skip (from "## Sequencing")

## From: 18-chunk-hardening.md

- `[ ]` Gap response deduplication signal — when user regenerates after answering a gap, the gap question may not reappear (requirement now PARTIAL/STRONG); UI should handle gracefully: "this requirement was previously a gap but your added experience resolved it" rather than the question silently disappearing (from "## Part 6 — Gaps and Edge Cases Worth Handling")
- `[ ]` Position ordering for user_input chunks — new user_input chunks should set `position = max(position for all source_types) + 1`; currently position resets to 0 on first chunk (from "## Part 6")
- `[ ]` Embed-before-re-enrich ordering — gap response endpoint must embed synchronously before calling `re_enrich_single_chunk`; otherwise new chunk has no vector and cannot be retrieved in top-K (from "## Part 6")
- `[~]` bfcache/pageShowKey in `TailoringDetail.tsx` — attempted (`pageShowKey` state + `pageshow` effect), then reverted same session; deferred to avoid bfcache edge cases (from "## Part 5 — Frontend: Tailoring Analysis UI Overhaul")

## From: 01-remaining-work-dump.md (carried forward — not yet completed)

Items dropped (completed per sprint plans): Remove legacy backend endpoints, Remove dead frontend routes, Rate limit soft warning, Race condition GitHub+resume, Internal admin page, Branch protection on main, Staging bootstrap, Design token text-link CSS var, GitHub deep crawl, Embeddings/pgvector, Profile snapshot (Level 2), Debug log table (Level 3), Eval pipeline (Level 4), Post-tailoring gap detection, Privacy policy, Terms of use, Path-specific CLAUDE.md rules, Post-edit lint hooks, Skills for repeated tasks (partial).

Items still open:

- `[ ]` Cloudflare API token — 1Password CLI injection: store `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` in 1Password, use `op run -- terraform apply`; same pattern for `TF_VAR_db_password`. No urgency while running from local machine. (Infra)
- `[ ]` Portfolio write-up `planning/12-portfolio-writeup.md` — same as sprint entries above (Tech Debt)
- `[ ]` Accessibility audit — missing `aria-label` on icon-only buttons, `role="alert"` on error states, keyboard nav through sidebar tailoring list (Tech Debt)
- `[ ]` Mobile layout — tailoring detail header with View Posting / Copy / Regenerate / Export buttons needs dropdown or different layout at small screens (Tech Debt)
- `[ ]` Backend test coverage to 80%+ — currently ~49% overall; remaining gap: SSE streaming, background tasks, Notion export, experience endpoints (Testing)
- `[~]` Frontend API route tests with next-test-api-route-handler — routes are thin proxies; revisit if routes gain meaningful logic (Testing, deferred)
- `[~]` VNet integration for PostgreSQL — security debt: public endpoint + `0.0.0.0/0.0.0.0` firewall rule; fix is destructive (recreate Container App Environments); +$10–15/month; deferred until compliance requirement (Infra, deferred)
- `[ ]` Separate Tailord Staging Google OAuth client — currently sharing prod OAuth client; create separate when there is a team with independent staging/prod access (Infra)
- `[ ]` SARIF-based CVE monitoring (when repo goes public) — Trivy SARIF upload to GitHub Security → Code scanning; free on public repos (Infra)
- `[ ]` Homepage ProductPreview — real screenshot (same as 04-github entry above)
- `[ ]` Evidence extraction architecture (two-phase) — Phase 1: single call extracts flat evidence claims from profile; Phase 2: scoring calls match requirements against evidence list; prevents inferred claims; prerequisite: eval pipeline ✓ (LLM Pipeline)
- `[ ]` Section pre-filtering — classify sections as evaluable/non-evaluable before chunk enrichment; skip "What We Offer", "Benefits", "About Us", "Compensation" etc.; eliminates false Gap scores for company perks (LLM Pipeline)
- `[ ]` Candidate fact sheet indexing pass — single LLM call before scoring produces compact "candidate fact sheet" from raw profile; all chunk scoring uses fact sheet instead of full profile; reduces context size (LLM Pipeline)
- `[ ]` Targeted self-verification on Gap scores — for chunks scored 0 on items likely to match (YOE, named technologies, education), run a second focused verification call: "Does the candidate meet this? yes/no + one sentence reason" (LLM Pipeline)
- `[ ]` Prompt iteration — systematically review `generate_tailoring` system prompt; add few-shot examples to profile extraction prompt; neither reviewed since early iterations (LLM Pipeline)
- `[ ]` Interactive tailoring format — alternate view re-rendering job posting as frame with candidate experience woven in; each requirement annotated inline with sourced evidence; prerequisite: mature static surface (Feature)
- `[ ]` Tailorings on public profile — `show_on_profile` third toggle per tailoring (distinct from `letter_public` / `posting_public`); candidate curates which tailorings appear on their profile (Feature)
- `[ ]` Notion database export mode — export to a Notion database (not just page); creates row with company, role, date, job URL properties; optional toggle after page export is stable (Feature)
- `[ ]` Interactive homepage demo — visitor pastes job URL on homepage → preview analysis without sign-up; blocked on guest/anonymous analysis flow (Feature, Day P4+)
- `[ ]` Homepage social proof section — "X tailorings generated", "X job requirements matched"; testimonials with concrete outcomes; blocked on real usage numbers (Feature)
- `[~]` Notion public integration review submission — deferred; submit once screenshots ready and usage justifies unlocking beyond 10 workspaces (Legal/Feature, deferred)
- `[ ]` Public profile chat interface — recruiter/interviewer asks questions on `/u/{slug}`, answered from candidate's structured experience; prerequisites: high-quality extracted profiles + hosted fast model + rate limiting; defer until static profile surface is mature (North Star)
- `[ ]` Headless enrichment API for job boards — `POST /enrich` with partner API key auth; synchronous or async job ID + poll/webhook; prerequisite: working B2C product with usage evidence (North Star)
- `[ ]` MCP server for Tailord — expose experience and tailoring data/actions as MCP tools; resources: tailorings, experience, analysis; tools: generate_tailoring, get_fit_analysis, export_to_notion (North Star)
- `[ ]` AI job search assistant features — cross-job intelligence: "which saved jobs best matches my skills?", "what skills am I missing most often?", "draft interview questions for {company}" (North Star)
- `[ ]` Platform partnership pitch to Simplify / Teal / Ashby — approach one platform with usage evidence; prerequisite: working B2C product with real users (North Star)
- `[ ]` OpenAPI schema cleanup — consistent response shapes, meaningful operation IDs, documented error codes; prerequisite for B2B integration partners (North Star)
- `[ ]` JSON endpoint for public tailoring/profile pages — `?format=json` or `Accept: application/json` on `/u/{slug}` and `/u/{slug}/{tailoringSlug}` (North Star)
- `[ ]` Webhooks for async enrichment events — `tailoring.ready`, `tailoring.enriched`, `experience.processed`; design with headless enrichment API in mind (North Star)
- `[ ]` Claude Code: Subagent definitions — `.claude/agents/` with `backend.md`, `frontend.md`, `reviewer.md` (security + correctness review, read-only) (Developer Workflow)
- `[ ]` Claude Code: GitHub MCP — configure GitHub MCP server for PR creation, issue viewing, CI status, PR comments; project-level `.mcp.json` (Developer Workflow)
- `[ ]` Claude Code: PostgreSQL MCP — configure PostgreSQL MCP for direct DB access to debug issues or verify migrations; keep credentials in personal `~/.claude.json` only (Developer Workflow)

## From: misc/geminiExchangePillars.md + misc/geminiExchangeExperience.md

*Items from Gemini planning sessions on the 6-pillar experience framework and the platform/integration architectural boundary. Cross-referenced against existing items above — only net-new items listed here.*

### Platform / Integration Architecture

- `[ ]` Platform/Integration boundary audit — review core platform models and FastAPI routes for dev-specific vocabulary that belongs in an integration spoke, not the core platform. Does `ExperienceChunk` implicitly assume software engineering (e.g. `source_type="github"`)? The core platform should abstractly be a claims ledger; developer-specific ingestion logic should be isolatable. Audit, document findings, decide what (if anything) to move now vs. defer.
- `[ ]` Universal atomic claims schema alignment — extend `ExperienceChunk` (or design a parallel model) to fully support the abstract schema: `{status, pillar, claim_text, metadata: {primary_tool, supporting_tools, industry_context}, provenance: {label, url}}`. Fields `pillar`, `provenance_url`, `provenance_label`, and explicit `status` (pending/approved/archived) are absent from the current model. Covers Alembic migration + Pydantic schema + API contract updates.

### Claim Approval Workflow

- `[ ]` Add `status` enum to `ExperienceChunk` — `pending / approved / archived` (Alembic migration). Claims from automated integrations (GitHub webhook, future connectors) start as `pending`. Only `approved` claims feed into the advocacy engine (tailoring generation, chunk scoring, profile snapshot).
- `[ ]` Claim review / reconciliation UI — lightweight ledger on the Experience page or a dedicated surface: pending claim text | inferred pillar | provenance link (external, outbound to source of truth — GitHub PR, ArtStation, etc.) | [Approve] [Edit] [Reject]. No in-app code viewers — all source links are outbound.
- `[ ]` Digest notification system — low-frequency background worker counting pending claims per user; sends a weekly summary: "You generated 3 new experience claims this week — review them here." Requires email or in-app notification infrastructure. Design schema and worker first; decide on delivery channel (Resend, in-app notification centre) separately.

### GitHub Integration — Silent Capture

- `[ ]` GitHub webhook endpoint (`POST /integrations/github/webhook`) — receive `push` and `pull_request` (merged) events; verify payload signature; enqueue background job to extract claims from PR title, description, and repo metadata (not raw code diffs). Creates `pending` ExperienceChunks. Upgrade path from current user-triggered enrichment to zero-overhead background capture.
- `[ ]` PR-description claim extraction — background agent processes merged PR metadata (title, description, labels, linked issues) and synthesizes atomic experience claims. No raw diff parsing — PR descriptions are human-readable and consistent with the multi-industry platform vision.

### 6 Product Pillars Framework

- `[ ]` Define and document the 6 Product Pillars schema for the dev integration — finalize pillar names and boundaries (draft from Gemini: Observability & Incident Response, Security/Identity/Trust, Reliability/Resilience/Performance, Developer Experience & Continuous Delivery, Architecture & Longevity, Usability & User Centricity). For each pillar: structural signatures a codebase scanner would look for; competency keywords it maps to in job descriptions. Store as a reference doc used by the GitHub enrichment agent prompt.
- `[ ]` Wire pillar classification into GitHub enrichment output — update `GitHubRepoEnrichment` LLM schema and prompt to output a `pillar` field per experience claim. Enriches claims with competency category for downstream JD matching.
- `[ ]` Add `pillar` key to `ExperienceChunk.chunk_metadata` — standardize the key for dev-integration-sourced claims; `chunk_metadata` JSON already exists.

### TAILORD.md Artifact

- `[ ]` Design TAILORD.md spec — a repo-level manifest (auto-generated by agentic scan) documenting a project's experience claims organized by pillar with links to source files/directories. Two-artifact model: `TAILORD.md` (product-level, public, checked in) describes the system; `.tailord/claims.json` (personal, gitignored) maps the specific user's commits to their contribution claims. Design schema before building.
- `[ ]` Build TAILORD.md for the Tailord repo itself — generate it even before the feature is user-facing, as a portfolio artifact and live demo of what the feature produces. "Eat your own cooking."
- `[ ]` Agentic TAILORD.md generation — background task that accepts a connected repo, runs a structural scan (directory layout, dependency manifests, CI config, observability setup), and produces a draft `TAILORD.md`. User reviews and approves in the dashboard; connects to the pending claims workflow.

### Firecrawl Investigation

- `[ ]` Investigate Firecrawl as alternative/supplement to current Playwright + BeautifulSoup job scraper — purpose-built to convert web pages (including SPAs) into clean LLM-ready markdown. Evaluate: (a) hosted API (cost vs. current Playwright infra complexity); (b) self-hosted open-source fork (adds operational overhead, no per-call cost). Key question: does Firecrawl handle ATS-hosted job postings (Greenhouse, Lever, Workday) better than the current extractor? Run against existing eval fixtures for comparison.

### Firecrawl Implementation

- `[ ]` Implement Firecrawl free tier as replacement/supplement to current Playwright scraper — not just evaluate. Hosted API free tier: 500 scrapes/month. Target: replace `backend/app/core/scraper.py` Playwright flow with Firecrawl for initial page fetch; keep BeautifulSoup parsing for content extraction. Test against Greenhouse, Lever, Workday ATS-hosted pages vs. existing eval fixtures.

### Auth & Onboarding

- `[ ]` [CONVERSATION TODO] Email infrastructure — evaluate free-tier transactional email providers (Resend, Postmark, SendGrid); design for: magic link auth, weekly claim digest, account welcome. Decide on provider before building.
- `[ ]` [CONVERSATION TODO] Magic link login — add email/magic-link as second auth method alongside Google OAuth; lowers barrier for non-Google users.
- `[ ]` [CONVERSATION TODO] LinkedIn OAuth + profile import — LinkedIn login as alternative to Google; primary value is importing initial professional profile (work history, education) as bootstrap for experience claims before resume upload.

### Claim Deduplication

- `[ ]` Claim deduplication — before inserting new ExperienceChunks from any integration, check for near-duplicate content against existing approved chunks for the same user. Prevent signal noise from generic claims repeating. Strategy: embedding cosine similarity threshold (≥0.92 = duplicate → skip or merge). Required prerequisite before enabling GitHub silent capture.
